title: Introduction to Physical Therapy
catalogDescription: Introduces the role of the physical therapist assistant in the practice of physical therapy with emphasis on the history of the profession, clinical practice, being a member of the healthcare team, PT/PTA relationship, ethics and professionalism, laws and regulations.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Acceptance into PTA program.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Patient Care Skills Part I
catalogDescription: Introduction to current best practice and skills related to preparation for patient care, body mechanics, positioning and draping, vital signs, infection control, wounds, emergencies, activities of daily living, and wheelchair features and activities.

Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Concurrent enrollment in PTA 101 and instructor consent. Student liability insurance required.

title: Fundamentals of Tests and Measures
catalogDescription: Covers the role of the physical therapist assistant in collecting subjective data, preparing for treatment, communication skills, performing and developing the skills of collecting objective data, and the clinical reasoning process. These tests and measures include mental functions, components of mobility and function, and musculoskeletal examination.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Acceptance into PTA program.

catalogDescription: Introduces normal body structure and function, pathological states and conditions, and underlying concepts of health and disease most commonly seen in the practice of physical therapy throughout the life span. title: Acute Care Lab
catalogDescription: Practice, discuss, implement and modify techniques encountered in the practice of physical therapy in acute care settings, including cardiac care, pulmonary care, burn care, skin care, post-operative conditions, and geriatrics.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Acceptance into PTA program.
Official Green River enrollment requirement text: PTA 130 with a grade of 2.0 or higher; and concurrent enrollment in PTA 212 .

title: Orthopedic Therapy Lab
catalogDescription: The study of therapeutic exercise, manual techniques, principles of tissue healing, orthopedic pathology and common diagnostic procedures in relationship to orthopedic conditions encountered in the practice of physical therapy.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Acceptance into PTA program.

title: Clinical Experience 1
catalogDescription: Supervised integrated clinical experience to introduce the student in the practice of physical therapy. Students are expected to actively participate in hands-on patient care, apply current knowledge and skills from coursework and build confidence within the clinical setting. This is a pass/no credit course.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Acceptance into PTA program.
Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Concurrent enrollment in PTA 251 .
Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Concurrent enrollment in PTA 252 .
